ogTitle: Fingal Head vs Firefly: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fingal Head and Firefly.
heading: Fingal Head vs Firefly — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fingal Head, New South Wales and Firefly, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fingal Head has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Firefly (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fingal Head or Firefly safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fingal Head has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Firefly scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fingal Head scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Firefly scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fingal Head or Firefly?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fingal Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
